commands.review_summary:
The PR changes update/status package-manager detection so `npm-shrinkwrap.json` is npm install evidence and the npm dependency lock marker, with tests for npm, pnpm, bun, and git/source precedence.

PR surface: Source +76, Tests +141. Total +217 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection plus the linked report gives a high-confidence reproduction path: a 2026.5.27 npm global install contains `npm-shrinkwrap.json` and pnpm build metadata, while current detection returns the package metadata manager first. I did not execute the repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is maintainer review of a draft, compatibility-sensitive update detection change.

target.title: fix(update): detect npm shrinkwrap package installs
target.body:
## Summary
- treat `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as npm install evidence for published npm package roots, even when package.json carries build-time `packageManager: pnpm`
- preserve pnpm and bun package-root ownership when their global-install layout proves they own a package root that also carries `npm-shrinkwrap.json`
- use `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm dependency lock marker when present so `openclaw status --json` does not report a missing pnpm/package-lock marker

Fixes #87732.

## Surface area
- No public config changes.
- No plugin API or plugin contract changes.
- No update command behavior rewrite; this only changes status/install detection and dependency marker reporting.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
A package-style OpenClaw install that contains `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, `node_modules`, and build-time package metadata should be reported using the package manager that owns the install root. npm shrinkwrap package roots should report npm, while pnpm and bun-owned package roots should remain pnpm or bun even when the published package also contains shrinkwrap.

Real environment tested:
WSL2 Ubuntu checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-proof-87778-shrinkwrap`, PR head `f858b61c1bfc`, source-level temp package roots using the real `detectPackageManager` and `checkUpdateStatus` code paths.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Built temp npm, pnpm, and bun package roots, each with `package.json`, `npm-shrinkwrap.json`, and dependency markers for its package manager.
2. Ran `detectPackageManager(root)` against each temp root.
3. Ran `checkUpdateStatus({ root, includeRegistry: false, fetchGit: false, timeoutMs: 1000 })` against each temp root.

Evidence after fix:
```json
{
  "status": "pass",
  "head": "f858b61c1bfc",
  "rootSuffix": "openclaw-87778-managers-Ymstiy",
  "managers": {
    "npm": {
      "detected": "npm",
      "installKind": "package",
      "packageManager": "npm",
      "deps": {
        "manager": "npm",
        "status": "ok",
        "lockfile": "npm-shrinkwrap.json",
        "marker": "node_modules"
      }
    },
    "pnpm": {
      "detected": "pnpm",
      "installKind": "package",
      "packageManager": "pnpm",
      "deps": {
        "manager": "pnpm",
        "status": "ok",
        "lockfile": "pnpm-lock.yaml",
        "marker": ".modules.yaml"
      }
    },
    "bun": {
      "detected": "bun",
      "installKind": "package",
      "packageManager": "bun",
      "deps": {
        "manager": "bun",
        "status": "ok",
        "lockfile": "bun.lockb",
        "marker": "node_modules"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The npm temp package root detected npm and used `npm-shrinkwrap.json` as the npm lockfile. The pnpm-owned temp package root detected pnpm and used pnpm dependency markers. The bun-owned temp package root detected bun and used bun dependency markers.

What was not tested:
No registry lookup was tested; this proof disables registry access with `includeRegistry: false` because the bug is local install/dependency-marker detection.

## Validation
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/infra/update-global.test.ts src/commands/status.update.test.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 5 files, 69 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/infra/update-global.ts src/infra/update-global.test.ts src/commands/status.update.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig ./tsconfig.json src/infra/detect-package-manager.ts src/infra/detect-package-manager.test.ts src/infra/update-check.ts src/infra/update-check.test.ts src/infra/update-global.ts src/infra/update-global.test.ts src/commands/status.update.ts src/commands/status-json-payload.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
